title: Crown Castle to Present at the Bank of America 2023 Media, Communications & Entertainment Conference
text: HOUSTON, Aug. 31, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Crown Castle Inc. (NYSE: CCI) ("Crown Castle") announced today that Dan Schlanger, Crown Castle's Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer is scheduled to present on Thursday, September 14, 2023 at 9:40 a.m. Eastern Time at the Bank of America 2023 Media, Communications & Entertainment Conference. The presentation will be broadcast live over the Internet and is expected to last approximately 40 minutes. The live audio webcast link and presentation for the conference will be available on Crown Castle's website at www.crowncastle.com, where it will also be archived for replay.

title: 3 Dividend Stocks Yielding 6%+ to Buy Hand Over Fist Right Now
text: Clearway Energy expects to increase its payout towards the upper end of its 5% to 8% annual target range through 2026. Crown Castle anticipates returning to 7% to 8% annual dividend growth beyond 2025.

title: Best REIT To Buy: American Tower Vs. Crown Castle
text: Cell tower REITs have historically been very good investments. But what's the best pick of the sector? I compare American Tower and Crown Castle and present my top pick.

title: REIT Fantasy Football: 11 Companies For Your Starting Lineup
text: REIT valuations are down and yields are up, making them attractive to income investors again. The article suggests a diversified REIT portfolio for beginners, with one REIT from each sector, to form a REIT fantasy football team. Different types of REITs serve different roles, including stable income, growth potential, and niche opportunities.

title: Crown Castle Vs. American Tower: Only One Deserves To Be Called King
text: American Tower and Crown Castle are both telecommunications companies that own and operate towers for data transmission. American Tower is larger in size with 226,000 towers compared to Crown Castle's 40,000 towers. Crown Castle is superior to American Tower in terms of profitability, yield, leverage, and valuation, making it a more compelling prospect.

title: Profit Potential: 5 Undervalued Stocks With High Dividend Yields
text: In some cases, it's even possible to offset losses elsewhere with dividends: A perfect example is the Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund NYSEARCA: XLU, whose price actually declined in 2022, but a healthy dividend yield meant the ETF returned 1.42% last year. Utilities was the only sector other than energy to post a gain in 2022.

title: Crown Castle to Present at the Goldman Sachs 2023 Communacopia & Technology Conference
text: HOUSTON, Aug. 23, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Crown Castle Inc. (NYSE: CCI) ("Crown Castle") announced today that Jay Brown, Crown Castle's Chief Executive Officer is scheduled to present on Wednesday, September 6, 2023 at 2:30 p.m. Eastern Time at the Goldman Sachs Communacopia & Technology Conference. Crown Castle's presentation will be broadcast live over the Internet and is expected to last approximately 40 minutes. The live audio webcast link and presentation for the conference will be available on Crown Castle's website at www.crowncastle.com.

title: Bloodshed On REIT Street: 5 High-Quality Landlords I'm Buying For The Long Haul
text: Larger REITs have outperformed small- and mid-sized REITs in the last five years, thanks partially to their concentration in high-growth sectors. Modestly leveraged REITs can still grow their bottom-line profits even with rapidly rising interest expenses because revenue is growing as well. I highlight five high-quality, larger market cap REITs that are currently trading at reasonable (or better-than-reasonable) valuations. I'm buying for the long haul.

title: There Might Not Be Another Chance Like This For Years
text: Today, you have a rare opportunity to win big with REITs. REITs have crashed even as their cash flows rose. Let others panic. I explain why this is an opportunity.

title: Losers Of REIT Earnings Season
text: After covering the Winners of REIT Earnings Season last week, Part 2 of our Earnings Recap focuses on the worst-performing property sectors and common threads shared by these laggards. While there were upside standouts and some solid reports within these lagging property sectors, the losers of REIT earnings season included: Self-Storage, Hotel, Healthcare, and Specialty REITs. Many of the "misses" emanated from the direct and secondary effects of the higher interest rate environment, underscoring the continued challenges facing more-highly-levered private real estate portfolios.

title: The 3 Most Undervalued 5G Stocks to Buy Now: August 2023
text: Fifth-generation wireless (5G) is becoming the global standard for wireless networks. But the full rollout of 5G will take several years – and billions of dollars.

title: Crown Castle: Buy The Dip And Start The DRIP
text: Crown Castle Inc. is undervalued and generating a high yield, making it a good candidate for dividend reinvestment. The company has a strong position in the U.S. cell tower market and reliable cash flow streams from major telecoms. While near-term growth may be limited, long-term prospects for fiber and tower revenue growth make it an attractive investment opportunity.

title: REITs Vs. BDCs: A Lesson In Contrarian Investing
text: REITs and BDCs are complementary assets that work well together in a dividend-focused portfolio. REITs are borrowers of debt, while BDCs are lenders of debt. Rising interest rates benefit BDCs and are a headwind for REITs, while falling interest rates have the opposite effect.

title: Who Else Is Doubling Down On Cell Tower REITs?
text: Real estate investment trusts offer steady dividend payments and are often considered "boring" compared to tech-focused companies. Cell tower REITs, data center REITs, and industrial/warehouse REITs offer more stock price growth potential than traditional landlords. American Tower and Crown Castle are two cell tower REITs that are currently underpriced and offer favorable risk/reward opportunities.

title: 50% Off and a 6% Dividend
text: A seven-day slide has real estate investment trust (REIT) Crown Castle NYSE: CCI trading 50% below its January 2021 record peak. The stock's forward dividend yield has doubled to 6%.
